page: Microsoft Azure B2as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the Basv2 server family. It is built on the x86_64 architecture and powered by an AMD EPYC 7763 processor running at 3.525 GHz. The instance features 1 physical core with 2 threads, presenting 2 vCPUs to the operating system, alongside 8.0 GB of system memory. This server type runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, does not include local storage, and is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are bundled with this configuration.

Benchmark data reveals a distinct contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The single-core CPU performance measured via stress-ng is strong, placing in the top 25% with a score of 2701.19. However, multi-core performance is weak, scoring 2701.19 in stress-ng and 2141.0 in Geekbench. Memory performance is a notable bottleneck; the Passmark memory score and small-block read-only memory bandwidth are poor, placing in the bottom 10%. Text processing and single-threaded compression tasks perform in the average range, while database operations and Redis SET operations show poor performance, scoring in the bottom 10%.

From a cost perspective, the burstable CPU allocation model offers a budget-friendly profile for workloads with variable CPU utilization. The primary tradeoff is the performance limitation under sustained multi-threaded workloads, as evidenced by poor software compilation and ray tracing benchmarks. This server is suited for low-traffic web serving, light text processing, and development or testing environments where continuous full CPU utilization is not required.
description: Microsoft Azure B2as_v2 is a burstable virtual machine from the Basv2 family, featuring an AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 2 vCPUs and 8.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, this instance lacks local storage but supports Premium Storage. Performance benchmarks show strong single-core CPU capabilities, but multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are weak to poor, with Passmark memory and database operations scoring in the bottom 10%. The burstable CPU allocation provides a cost-efficient profile for workloads with variable resource demands. This server is suited for light workloads such as low-traffic web serving, basic text processing, and development environments that do not require sustained multi-threaded compute power.

tagline: A burstable virtual machine featuring AMD EPYC processing and balanced memory for cost-conscious, variable-demand workloads.

Burstable CPU allocation for cost-efficient variable workloads
